page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Interstitial Cystitis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.

natural_products.name: Taurine
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Taurine protects against degenerative changes in the urinary bladder, suggesting its role in the treatment of interstitial cystitis.

supporting_publications.title: Taurine protects against degenerative changes in the urinary bladder, suggesting its role in the treatment of interstitial cystitis.

natural_products.name: L-Arginine
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Long-term oral administration of L-arginine increases nitric oxide related enzymes and metabolites in the urine of patients with interstitial cysti; Human Study: Long-term oral L-arginine appears to improve interstitial cystitis related symptoms.

literature.title: A randomized double-blind placebo-controlled crossover trial of the efficacy of L-arginine in the treatment of interstitial cystitis
literature.authors: Cartledge JJ, Davies AM, Eardley I

literature.title: A randomized double-blind trial of oral L-arginine for treatment of interstitial cystitis
literature.authors: Korting GE, Smith SD, Wheeler MA et al.

supporting_publications.title: A randomized double-blind placebo-controlled crossover trial of the efficacy of L-arginine in the treatment of interstitial cystitis
A randomized double-blind trial of oral L-arginine for treatment of interstitial cystitis

Long-term oral administration of L-arginine increases nitric oxide related enzymes and metabolites in the urine of patients with interstitial cystitis, which is associated with a decrease in interstitial cystitis related symptoms.

natural_products.name: Chondroitin

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Partial spontaneous remission in 20-30% at 2 years; complete resolution rare; chronic pain condition with significant disability; no biomarker-defined diagnosis complicates studies
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Multimodal approach (dietary modification + physical therapy + neuromodulation + intravesical therapy): 50-70% overall treatment response but rarely complete remission; intravesical heparin + lidocaine + sodium bicarbonate: 70-80% short-term response; sacral neuromodulation: 48-60% significant improvement at 2 years; cyclosporine A (oral): 75% improvement in Hunner lesion IC vs 19% placebo
remission.gap_size: Very large — no FDA-approved treatment; management is symptomatic and highly individualised
remission.barrier_detail: Only pentosan polysulfate sodium (Elmiron, FDA approved 1996) has FDA approval for IC/BPS, and this is now associated with maculopathy (pigmentary maculopathy) with long-term use; Hunner lesion IC (25% of cases) is a distinct pathological entity with different treatment response than non-Hunner IC; diagnostic heterogeneity makes clinical trials extremely difficult
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — AUA IC/BPS guideline updated; Elmiron maculopathy warning strengthened; no new pharmacotherapy approval; pentosan polysulfate sodium only approved drug since 1996
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term pentosan polysulfate sodium without annual ophthalmology monitoring (maculopathy risk — discontinuation recommended if changes detected); intravesical DMSO without individualised assessment (effective in non-Hunner IC, potentially harmful in Hunner lesion IC); hydraulic distension (high-pressure) as diagnostic-therapeutic procedure — evidence weak, risk of bladder rupture
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Low-acid diet (IC diet — reduces urinary pH and bladder wall irritation; widely recommended though RCT evidence limited); pelvic floor physical therapy (strong evidence for IC/BPS with pelvic floor tension myalgia component); quercetin (anti-inflammatory supplement — small RCT positive); amitriptyline for central sensitisation component
remission.frontier_signal: Cystoscopic fulguration of Hunner lesions (significant improvement in Hunner IC — gold standard for that subtype; expanding); AZD8835 (PI3Kδ/γ inhibitor Phase 2 for Hunner IC); spinal cord stimulation; CGRP antagonists for visceral pain sensitisation; stem cell therapy for glycosaminoglycan layer regeneration
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
